rationale: Sprott et al. (2009) is cited not only as general background on loyalty outcomes but also to justify a concrete operationalization choice in the experiments. In Studies 3a and 3b, the authors explicitly rely on that prior finding to treat willingness to buy other/new products from the same brand as evidence of brand loyalty. Because the citation informs how a key dependent variable is constructed and interpreted, it plays a meaningful functional role beyond a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Sprott et al. (2009)'s loyalty logic to its own context by using cross-category/new-product purchase intention from the same brand as a loyalty indicator. This prior work is used to justify and interpret the loyalty measures in the paper's experimental studies.
